sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The N container frame click had no gameplay effect, but the Y container frame click DID change something: yellow source Y went from 14px to 5px!
So clicking on the Y container frame (node 18) toggled the yellow source from 14px to 5px. This is different from clicking destination Y shapes which toggle between 23px and 14px.
Wait, let me reconsider. The yellow source was at 14px (partial state from previous toggles). After clicking the Y container frame, it went to 5px. So the container frame click seems to toggle to a DIFFERENT state.
Let me track the yellow source states:
- Full: 23px
- Partial 1: 14px (toggled by clicking destination Y shapes)
- Partial 2: 5px (toggled by clicking Y container frame?)
This is a new discovery! The container frames DO have an effect, but only on the Y container. Let me check if the N container frame also has an effect on the green source.
But wait, I clicked the N container frame at (18, 36) and it didn't change the N pixels. Let me check if I clicked the right position. Node 9 has boundary [[18, 36], [18, 48], [24, 48], [24, 36]]. So (18, 36) is the top-left corner of the N container frame. Maybe that position is on the corner and didn't register properly?
Actually, looking more carefully, the N source was at 5px before and after the N container frame click. So either the N container frame doesn't toggle the green source, or I need to click a different position on the frame.
Let me now focus on the yellow source. It went from 14px to 5px. What if I click the Y container frame again to toggle it back? And what if I click the N container frame at a different position?
Also, I notice the current state:
- Green N: 5px
- Yellow Y: 5px
Both sources are at 5px now. What if the goal is to make both sources match (both at 5px)?
Let me try clicking the N container frame at a different position to see if it also toggles the green source.

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# IMPORTANT DISCOVERY: Clicking Y container frame (35, 21) changed yellow source from 14px to 5px!
# This is a new toggle state. Let me explore this further.
# Current state: green N=5px, yellow Y=5px

# First, let me try clicking the N container frame at a different position
# Node 9 boundary: [[18, 36], [18, 48], [24, 48], [24, 36]]
# Try the right edge of the frame
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 48}])
print("Clicked N container frame right edge (21, 48):")
print("Result:", last_action_result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation
n_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'}
y_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}
print(f"N pixels: {n_pixels}")
print(f"Y pixels: {y_pixels}")

# If that doesn't work, try clicking Y container frame again to see if it toggles back
if n_pixels.get(2, 5) == 5:  # If green still at 5px
    print("\nN frame click had no effect. Trying Y frame again...")
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 47, 'col': 21}])
    print("Clicked Y container frame bottom (47, 21):")
    print("Result:", last_action_result)
    
    seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
    n_pixels2 = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'}
    y_pixels2 = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}
    print(f"N pixels: {n_pixels2}")
    print(f"Y pixels: {y_pixels2}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
